title: Letter Matching Game
description: Match uppercase letters to lowercase letters or letters to their sounds

detailed_specs.objective: Help children recognize letters in both uppercase and lowercase forms, and associate letters with their phonetic sounds

gameplay_mechanics.setup: Display a grid of face-down cards (4x4 for beginner, 5x4 for intermediate, 6x4 for advanced)
gameplay_mechanics.interaction: Click/tap to flip two cards at a time

matching_rules.beginner: Match uppercase to lowercase of same letter (A-a, B-b, etc.)
matching_rules.intermediate: Match letter to picture of word starting with that sound (M to moon, S to sun)
matching_rules.advanced: Match letter to its phonetic sound written out (/m/, /s/, etc.)

feedback.correct_match: Play sound effect, cards stay revealed, display encouraging message ('Great job!', 'That's right!')
feedback.incorrect_match: Cards flip back over after 1 second, play gentle error sound
feedback.completion: Celebration animation, display time taken and number of attempts, option to play again or increase difficulty

visual_design.card_front: Colorful solid background with large, clear letter or image
visual_design.card_back: Consistent pattern (stars, abc pattern, or fun graphic)
visual_design.color_scheme: Bright primary colors, high contrast for readability
visual_design.animations: Smooth flip animation (CSS 3D transform), bounce effect on match, confetti on completion

audio_elements.letter_sounds: Play phonetic sound when letter card is revealed (clear pronunciation)
audio_elements.background_music: Optional soft, playful background music (can be toggled)
audio_elements.sound_effects: Match success (chime), mismatch (soft boop), game complete (fanfare)

accessibility.keyboard_navigation: Arrow keys to move between cards, Enter to flip
accessibility.screen_reader: Announce card position and content when focused
accessibility.color_blind_mode: Add symbols/patterns in addition to colors for differentiation
accessibility.difficulty_scaling: Adjustable grid size, option for fewer cards

technical_implementation.html_structure: Grid container with card divs, each containing front/back faces
technical_implementation.css_requirements: Flexbox/Grid for layout, 3D transforms for flip, transitions for smooth animations
technical_implementation.javascript_logic: Card shuffle algorithm, match detection, timer, score tracking, state management
technical_implementation.data_structure: Array of letter objects with uppercase, lowercase, sound, and image properties

progression_system.stars_earned: 3 stars for <10 attempts, 2 stars for <15 attempts, 1 star for completion
progression_system.unlockables: Complete beginner to unlock intermediate, complete intermediate to unlock advanced
progression_system.achievements: Perfect game (no mistakes), speed run (<30 seconds), all levels complete

educational_value.parent_tips: Start with just a few letter pairs. Let your child say the letter names and sounds aloud. Praise effort, not just correct answers.

title: Word Builder
description: Drag and drop letters to build CVC words, then blend to hear the word

detailed_specs.objective: Build decoding and spelling skills by constructing words from individual letter sounds

setup.word_target: Display image of target word (e.g., picture of a cat)
setup.letter_bank: Provide letter tiles at bottom of screen (drag source)
setup.word_spaces: Three (or more) blank spaces where letters should be dropped
setup.sound_buttons: Each letter tile plays its sound when clicked

interaction.drag_drop: Click/touch and drag letter tiles to appropriate spaces
interaction.snap_to_grid: Letters snap into position when dropped in correct area
interaction.remove_letter: Drag letter back to bank or click 'X' button to remove
interaction.blend_button: After all spaces filled, 'Blend It!' button appears

word_checking.real_time: Each dropped letter is read aloud (phonetic sound)
word_checking.blend_mode: Clicking 'Blend It!' smoothly reads the whole word
word_checking.correct_word: Image animates, word is displayed, congratulatory message, word added to 'words mastered' list
word_checking.incorrect_word: Gentle feedback ('That spells [wrong word]. Try again!'), option to hear sounds again

progression.word_queue: Series of 8-10 words per session, progress bar shows completion
progression.increasing_difficulty: Start with 3-letter CVC, gradually add blends, digraphs, silent e

visual_design.layout: Target image at top, word-building area in center, letter bank at bottom
visual_design.letter_tiles: Large, colorful tiles with clear sans-serif font (consonants blue, vowels red)
visual_design.word_spaces: Outlined boxes with dashed borders, numbered (1, 2, 3) for sequencing
visual_design.animations: Tiles wiggle when incorrect, bounce when correct, smooth slide into position

audio_elements.letter_sounds: Individual phoneme sounds for each letter
audio_elements.blending: Smooth continuous blending when 'Blend It!' is pressed (e.g., /mmm/ /aaa/ /ttt/ = mat)
audio_elements.whole_word: Clear pronunciation of complete word after successful build
audio_elements.encouragement: Randomized praise phrases ('Excellent!', 'You did it!', 'Super work!')

difficulty_progression.level_1_CVC: 3-letter words with short vowels (cat, dog, sun, pig)
difficulty_progression.level_4_CVCe: Silent e words (cake, bike, rope, cube)
difficulty_progression.level_5_digraphs: Words with ch, sh, th (chip, shop, that)

hint_system.first_letter_hint: Highlights first letter after 2 incorrect attempts
hint_system.sound_all_letters: Button to hear all sounds in sequence
hint_system.show_word: After 3 incorrect attempts, option to see word spelled out with animation

technical_implementation.html_structure: Draggable letter divs, droppable word space divs, target image container
technical_implementation.css_requirements: Drag-and-drop styling, tile colors, animations (keyframes), responsive grid

javascript_logic.drag_drop_api: HTML5 drag-and-drop or touch events for mobile
javascript_logic.word_validation: Compare built word against correct answer
javascript_logic.audio_playback: Web Audio API or Howler.js for sound management
javascript_logic.state_management: Track current word, attempts, completed words, mastery level

technical_implementation.data_structure: JSON object with word, image path, phonemes array, difficulty level, category

parent_controls.word_lists: Customize word list (focus on specific phonics patterns)
parent_controls.progress_tracking: View words mastered, accuracy rate, time spent

educational_value.parent_tips: Encourage your child to say each sound aloud as they drag letters. Model blending by running your finger under the letters as you say the sounds smoothly. Celebrate spelling success!

title: Sight Word Flash
description: Fast-paced sight word recognition game with multiple game modes

detailed_specs.objective: Build automatic recognition of high-frequency sight words through repeated exposure and practice

flash_cards.description: Traditional flashcard practice with customizable timing

mechanics.display: Show word for set duration (3 seconds for beginners, 1 second for advanced)
mechanics.response: Child reads word aloud, parent/system confirms correct or incorrect
mechanics.flip: Click to see word again, arrow to next word

settings.auto_advance: Toggle automatic progression or manual control
settings.word_list: Select from Dolch, Fry, or custom word lists

word_pop.description: Pop bubbles containing target sight word before they float away

mechanics.target_word: Announce/display target word at top of screen
mechanics.bubbles: Multiple bubbles float up screen, each containing a word
mechanics.gameplay: Click/tap only bubbles with the target word
mechanics.scoring: Points for correct pops, penalty for wrong pops, combo multiplier for consecutive correct
mechanics.challenge: Speed increases as level progresses, distractor words become more similar

visual_design.bubbles: Translucent colorful bubbles with words inside
visual_design.background: Underwater or sky theme with gentle animation
visual_design.effects: Bubble pop animation, splash effect, score indicators floating up

word_race.description: Race against time to identify as many sight words as possible

mechanics.word_display: Large word appears in center of screen
mechanics.buttons: Multiple choice buttons with word pronunciations
mechanics.scoring: 1 point per correct answer, streak bonus for consecutive correct
mechanics.leaderboard: Track high scores and improvement over time

difficulty_scaling.easy: 4 clearly different word choices, 5 seconds per word
difficulty_scaling.medium: 4 similar-looking word choices, 3 seconds per word
difficulty_scaling.hard: 6 choices including common confusions (there/their), 2 seconds per word

memory_match.description: Match sight words to pictures/definitions or duplicate word pairs

mechanics.matching: Pair sight words with images showing their meaning, or match duplicate words
mechanics.feedback: Word is read aloud when flipped

visual_design.typography: Large, clear sans-serif font (minimum 48px), excellent contrast ratio
visual_design.color_coding: Optional color coding for word types (nouns, verbs, etc.)
visual_design.themes: Selectable visual themes (ocean, space, jungle, rainbow) to maintain engagement
visual_design.animations: Minimal distracting elements, smooth transitions, clear feedback

audio_elements.word_pronunciation: Clear audio recording of each sight word

progress_tracking.mastery_criteria: Word is 'mastered' after 3 consecutive correct rapid recognitions
progress_tracking.review_schedule: Spaced repetition algorithm brings back previously mastered words
progress_tracking.visual_progress: Progress bars, badges for milestones (10 words mastered, 50 words, etc.)

detailed_stats.words_mastered: List of mastered words with dates
detailed_stats.accuracy_rate: Percentage correct overall and per word list

technical_implementation.html_structure: Game container, word display area, button/bubble containers, progress indicators

css_requirements.animations: CSS animations for bubbles floating, words appearing/disappearing
css_requirements.responsive: Adapt layout for mobile, tablet, desktop
css_requirements.accessibility: High contrast mode, large touch targets (minimum 44px)

javascript_logic.spaced_repetition: Algorithm to optimize review schedule based on performance

data_structure.word_object: { word: string, list: string, mastered: boolean, attempts: [], lastSeen: date }
data_structure.session_data: { date: date, words_practiced: [], accuracy: number, duration: number }

parent_dashboard.overview: Snapshot of child's sight word mastery
parent_dashboard.recommendations: Suggested focus words based on performance
parent_dashboard.printables: Generate flashcard PDF for offline practice
parent_dashboard.settings: Customize word lists, difficulty, game modes available

educational_value.research_basis: Based on Dolch and Fry high-frequency word lists, which comprise 50-75% of all words in children's reading materials
educational_value.parent_tips: Practice daily for best results. These words often can't be 'sounded out' - they must be recognized by sight. Use words in sentences to build comprehension. Celebrate progress, not perfection!

title: Spelling Practice
description: Interactive spelling game with audio prompts and visual feedback

detailed_specs.objective: Develop phonemic awareness and spelling skills through auditory-visual-motor practice

listen_and_spell.description: Hear a word and spell it using on-screen keyboard or letter tiles

feedback.letter_by_letter: Each correct letter turns green as typed
feedback.incorrect_letter: Turns yellow, shakes slightly, can be backspaced
feedback.completion: Word is pronounced, animated correctly, meaning shown

hint_system.show_image: Display picture clue of the word
hint_system.first_letter: Reveal first letter after 1 incorrect attempt
hint_system.vowel_highlight: Highlight vowel positions after 2 incorrect attempts
hint_system.sound_it_out: Play word in slow, segmented phonemes

missing_letter.description: Fill in the missing letter(s) in partially completed words

mechanics.display: Word shown with blank spaces (e.g., c_t, s_op, _rain)
mechanics.selection: Click correct letter to fill blank
mechanics.difficulty: Progressively remove more letters or require more complex vowel/blend choices

word_scramble.description: Unscramble mixed-up letters to form correct word

mechanics.scrambled_tiles: Letters of word presented in random order

dictation.description: Write/spell sentences with multiple target words

mechanics.text_input: Full sentence input field or word-by-word boxes
mechanics.checking: Highlight correctly spelled words, offer corrections for errors

dictation.difficulty: Start with 3-4 word sentences, progress to 8-10 words

word_selection.phonics_pattern_based: Words grouped by phonics pattern being practiced (CVC, CVCe, blends, etc.)
word_selection.frequency_based: Include high-frequency words appropriate to level
word_selection.thematic: Optional themed word lists (animals, food, weather, etc.)
word_selection.custom: Parent/teacher can create custom word lists

layout.center: Main spelling area (keyboard, tiles, or input field)
layout.bottom: Hint buttons, repeat word button, submit button

color_scheme.incorrect_letter: Yellow background with gentle shake animation
color_scheme.keyboard: Bright, colorful keys with clear labels

visual_design.typography: Large, dyslexia-friendly font (OpenDyslexic or Comic Sans as options)
visual_design.animations: Letter pop-in effects, word completion celebration, smooth transitions

audio_elements.word_pronunciation: Professional voice recording for each word (clear, natural speech)
audio_elements.letter_sounds: Individual letter sounds when clicked on keyboard
audio_elements.encouragement_phrases: Randomized verbal encouragement ('Keep going!', 'Great try!', 'You've got this!')

difficulty_progression.level_1_CVC: Three-letter short vowel words (cat, dog, sun, bed)
difficulty_progression.level_2_CCVC_CVCC: Words with blends (stop, fast, club, help)
difficulty_progression.level_3_digraphs: sh, ch, th, wh words (shop, chip, that, when)
difficulty_progression.level_4_CVCe: Silent e words (cake, bike, rope, cute)
difficulty_progression.level_5_vowel_teams: ai, ay, ee, ea, oa words (rain, play, tree, boat)
difficulty_progression.level_6_r_controlled: ar, or, er, ir, ur words (car, fork, her, bird, turn)
difficulty_progression.level_7_complex: Multi-syllable, irregular, advanced patterns (happy, light, because)

adaptive_learning.error_analysis: Track which phonics patterns cause most difficulty
adaptive_learning.targeted_practice: Automatically generate practice sets focusing on weak areas
adaptive_learning.mastery_threshold: Word must be spelled correctly 3 times (not in a row) to mark as mastered
adaptive_learning.review_cycle: Bring back challenging words at increasing intervals (1 day, 3 days, 1 week)

accessibility_features.text_to_speech: All on-screen text can be read aloud
accessibility_features.keyboard_navigation: Full keyboard control (tab, enter, arrow keys)
accessibility_features.screen_reader: Compatible with screen readers, proper ARIA labels

adjustable_settings.timer: Option to practice without time pressure

html_structure.container: Main game container with sections for display, input, controls
html_structure.keyboard: Button grid for on-screen keyboard (QWERTY layout)
html_structure.input_display: Div or input field showing letters as typed

css_requirements.layout: CSS Grid for keyboard, Flexbox for overall structure
css_requirements.animations: Keyframe animations for letter entry, shake effects, celebrations
css_requirements.responsive: Media queries for mobile (larger touch targets), tablet, desktop
css_requirements.themes: CSS variables for easy theme switching

javascript_logic.word_checking: Compare user input to correct spelling, letter-by-letter validation
javascript_logic.audio_management: Preload audio files, manage playback queue
javascript_logic.progress_tracking: LocalStorage or IndexedDB to save progress
javascript_logic.spaced_repetition: Leitner system or SM-2 algorithm for review scheduling

data_structure.word_database: { word: string, phonics_pattern: string, difficulty: number, audio_url: string, image_url: string }
data_structure.user_progress: { word_id: string, attempts: number, correct_count: number, last_attempted: date, mastered: boolean }

performance_optimization.minification: Minimize JS/CSS for fast load times

progress_reports.summary_view: Words attempted, accuracy rate, patterns mastered
progress_reports.export: Download CSV or PDF report for sharing/records

custom_word_lists.creation: Interface to add custom words, assign difficulty, upload audio
custom_word_lists.assignment: Assign specific word lists to child
custom_word_lists.sharing: Share custom lists with other parents/teachers

educational_value.research_basis: Based on Orton-Gillingham approach and structured literacy principles - explicit, systematic phonics instruction combined with multisensory engagement
educational_value.parent_tips: Encourage 'sounding out' words before spelling. Praise effort and improvement, not just correct answers. Practice challenging words in short, frequent sessions rather than long, infrequent ones. Use a whiteboard or paper alongside the digital game for kinesthetic reinforcement.

title: Phonics Quiz
description: Comprehensive phonics assessment and practice covering all phonics concepts

detailed_specs.objective: Assess and reinforce phonics knowledge through varied question types, providing targeted feedback and adaptive difficulty

sound_identification.description: Identify the sound a letter or letter combination makes
sound_identification.example: What sound does 'ch' make? A) /k/ B) /s/ C) /ch/ D) /sh/
sound_identification.audio: Option to hear sounds for each choice

beginning_sound.description: Identify which word starts with a given sound
beginning_sound.format: Show 3-4 images, child selects one matching the prompt
beginning_sound.example: Which picture starts with /b/? [images: ball, cat, dog, fish]
beginning_sound.audio: Target sound played before images shown

word_reading.description: Read a word and select matching image
word_reading.example: Which picture matches the word 'cat'? [images of cat, hat, bat, mat]
word_reading.difficulty_scaling: Use visually similar choices for advanced (cat/hat look similar)

rhyming_words.format: Show/say 3 words, child picks the two that rhyme
rhyming_words.example: Which two rhyme? cat, dog, hat
rhyming_words.audio: Words spoken aloud for auditory learners

blend_segmentation.description: Break word into sounds or blend sounds together
blend_segmentation.format_segment: Hear word 'cat', select how many sounds (/k/ /a/ /t/ = 3)
blend_segmentation.format_blend: Hear sounds /s/ /u/ /n/, select which word it makes (sun)
blend_segmentation.visual_aid: Sound boxes or phoneme counters displayed

vowel_identification.description: Determine if vowel is short or long, or select correct vowel
vowel_identification.format: Read word 'cake', is the 'a' short or long?
vowel_identification.example: Fill in the blank: c__ke (options: a, a_e, ai, ay)
vowel_identification.difficulty: Progress from simple short/long distinction to choosing correct spelling pattern

pattern_recognition.description: Identify phonics pattern in a word
pattern_recognition.format: What pattern do you see in 'night'? A) CVC B) -ight C) CVCe D) blend
pattern_recognition.examples: Progress from basic CVC to complex patterns (diphthongs, r-controlled, etc.)

word_sort.format: Drag words into correct category buckets
word_sort.example: Sort these words: Short A | Long A → (cat, cake, rain, mat)
word_sort.interaction: Drag and drop or click to assign

features.hints_available: Show first letter, play word slowly, show picture clue
features.no_penalty: Incorrect answers don't affect score, opportunity to try again
features.review: After each question, option to review the concept

test_mode.description: Timed assessment to gauge current level

features.timer: Set time limit per question (10-30 seconds) or overall (10 minutes)
features.no_retries: Move to next question after answering
features.final_report: Detailed results with strengths and areas for improvement

mechanics.difficulty_adjustment: Correct answer → slightly harder next question; Incorrect → slightly easier
mechanics.targeted_questions: Focus on patterns child is close to mastering

features.lives_system: Start with 3 lives, lose one for each error
features.streak_bonus: Points multiplier for consecutive correct answers
features.leaderboard: Compare scores with previous attempts or family members
features.unlockables: Earn badges and rewards for high scores

layout.header: Progress bar showing question number, timer (if applicable), lives/score
layout.question_area: Large, clear display of question text and/or images
layout.answer_area: Multiple choice buttons or drag-drop zones
layout.footer: Hint button, quit button, sound toggle

color_coding.incorrect_answer: Red highlight with X icon, then show correct answer in green
color_coding.neutral_state: Soft blue or gray for unselected options

animations.question_transition: Smooth fade or slide between questions
animations.answer_feedback: Bounce/pulse animation for correct, shake for incorrect
animations.progress: Filling progress bar, confetti for completion

visual_design.mascot: Optional friendly character that gives encouragement (customizable avatar)

audio_elements.answer_options: Click to hear answer choices spoken
audio_elements.phoneme_sounds: Accurate phonetic sounds for all phonics elements

feedback_audio.explanation: Brief audio explanation of correct answer

audio_elements.background_music: Optional light, non-distracting background music (toggle on/off)

content_coverage.level_1_letter_sounds: All 26 letter sounds, focus on consonants and short vowels
content_coverage.level_2_CVC: Reading and building CVC words, identifying short vowel sounds
content_coverage.level_3_blends: Initial and final consonant blends (st, bl, nd, mp, etc.)
content_coverage.level_4_digraphs: sh, ch, th, wh, ck, ng recognition and usage
content_coverage.level_5_long_vowels: CVCe patterns, vowel teams (ai, ay, ee, ea, oa, ow, oo)
content_coverage.level_6_r_controlled: ar, or, er, ir, ur identification
content_coverage.level_7_advanced: Diphthongs (oi, oy, ou, ow), -igh, complex patterns
content_coverage.level_8_multisyllabic: Breaking longer words into syllables, compound words

adaptive_algorithm.item_response_theory: Questions have difficulty ratings, adjust based on student ability estimate

performance_tracking.correct_rate: Track percentage correct per phonics pattern
performance_tracking.speed: Monitor response time to gauge automaticity
performance_tracking.error_patterns: Identify systematic errors (e.g., confusing b/d, short/long vowels)

question_selection.spacing: Don't repeat same question type consecutively
question_selection.review_integration: Periodically include previously mastered concepts for retention
question_selection.targeted_practice: Emphasize patterns with 50-80% accuracy (zone of proximal development)

immediate_feedback.correct: Positive reinforcement, brief explanation why answer is right
immediate_feedback.incorrect: Show correct answer, explain the phonics rule, offer to review concept

question_explanation.concept_review: 'The 'sh' digraph makes one sound: /sh/ like in ship'

end_of_quiz_summary.strengths: Patterns mastered or performed well on
end_of_quiz_summary.recommendations: Suggested lessons or games to work on weak areas
end_of_quiz_summary.progress_comparison: How this compares to previous attempts (improvement tracking)

points_system.streak_bonus: 5 extra points for each consecutive correct (up to 5x multiplier)
points_system.speed_bonus: Bonus points for quick answers (optional, not in practice mode)

unlockables.avatar_accessories: Earn hats, glasses, backgrounds for avatar

progress_visualization.skill_tree: Visual map of phonics skills, unlock new branches as you progress
progress_visualization.certificate: Printable certificate when level is completed

html_structure.answer_options: Buttons or interactive elements for answers

css_requirements.responsive_design: Mobile-first design, adapt for tablets and desktops
css_requirements.animations: CSS transitions and keyframes for smooth UX
css_requirements.accessibility: High contrast, large touch targets, clear focus states
css_requirements.themes: CSS custom properties for theme switching

javascript_logic.question_engine: Randomize questions from pool, track answered questions
javascript_logic.scoring_system: Calculate scores, track streaks, update progress
javascript_logic.data_persistence: Save progress to localStorage or backend API
javascript_logic.analytics: Track detailed usage and performance metrics

data_structures.question_object: { id, type, text, audio_url, image_url, options: [{text, correct, explanation}], difficulty, phonics_pattern }
data_structures.quiz_session: { user_id, quiz_type, questions: [question_ids], answers: [{question_id, user_answer, correct, time_taken}], score, start_time, end_time }
data_structures.user_progress: { phonics_patterns: [{pattern, mastered, accuracy, last_practiced}], badges_earned, total_points }

api_integration.authentication: Optional user accounts for progress syncing across devices
api_integration.leaderboards: Optional social features for family/classroom competition

performance_overview.skill_breakdown: Visual representation (chart/graph) of strength in each phonics area

detailed_reports.by_pattern: Performance on each phonics pattern with examples

recommendations.focus_areas: Specific phonics patterns to practice next
recommendations.suggested_activities: Links to relevant lessons, games, or external resources
recommendations.intervention_alerts: Flagged if student is struggling significantly (< 40% on multiple attempts)

customization.quiz_creation: Create custom quizzes focused on specific patterns
customization.difficulty_settings: Lock certain difficulty levels or patterns

educational_value.session_duration: 10-20 minutes for practice, 15-30 minutes for full assessment
educational_value.research_basis: Aligned with National Reading Panel recommendations and Common Core phonics standards. Incorporates formative assessment principles for effective learning measurement.
educational_value.parent_tips: Use practice mode for daily skill building and test mode periodically (weekly/monthly) to track progress. Celebrate growth over time, not just high scores. Use the detailed reports to identify which patterns need more focus, then supplement with targeted lessons or activities. Encourage your child to explain WHY an answer is correct to deepen understanding.
